items.label: caregiver-friendly case intake front end
items.goal: Design the first public-facing but privacy-preserving case-intake surface so a caregiver can enter a loved one's multiple myeloma details through a simple guided flow, with all real case data routed only to a private, consented, access-controlled system and with no medical advice or public PHI.
requirements: Public repo may document UI, schemas, copy, synthetic examples, and private handoff contracts only; no real patient data, uploaded records, private notes, or identifying details belong in public artifacts, issues, logs, or examples.

The experience must be easy for a caregiver: plain-language questions, progressive sections, save/resume concept, unknown/not sure choices, unit/date helpers, and a final review screen before submission.

Define minimum viable case data and optional deeper data: diagnosis context, disease state, prior therapies, response/MRD history, relapse history, key labs with units and dates, cytogenetics/genomics if known, imaging/bone/renal/infection context, transplant/CAR T/bispecific exposure, current care-team questions, goals, and constraints.
Every field must carry provenance, date or timepoint where relevant, unknown/uncertain states, and a public-safe projection rule for downstream artifacts.
Include consent, privacy, security, data-minimization, retention, emergency, and clinician-review boundaries before any real intake implementation.
The system must not provide diagnosis, treatment recommendations, trial recommendations, prognosis, monitoring advice, or urgency guidance from the public interface.
Output should connect to the existing case-to-cure pipeline, case-feature bundle boundary, publication gate, and private-lab blocker model.
success_criteria: A public-safe intake roadmap or product spec exists and is linked from the frontier roadmap or case-to-cure plumbing artifacts.
A caregiver-friendly field inventory distinguishes required, optional, unknown, and private-only fields without collecting real data.
A synthetic-only intake example or fixture demonstrates the flow and validates without resembling a real person.
Privacy and clinical boundary copy is explicit enough that caregiver-like caregivers understand what the system can and cannot do.
Clawdad can hand off to the next build step without exposing PHI or implying medical advice.
plans: Start with a no-PHI public product/spec artifact before building any real data-capture backend.
Then define a private intake schema and public-safe projection contract.
Then prototype the front end only against synthetic fixtures.

items.label: Next public-safe caregiver intake build slice
items.goal: Extend the Caregiver Case Intake Product Spec v0 into one concrete public-safe next artifact: private-intake schema contract, public-safe projection validator, no-PHI/no-advice checklist, synthetic fixture expansion, or static synthetic prototype plan.
requirements: Start from disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/case-intake/caregiver-case-intake-product-spec-v0.md and examples/multiple-myeloma-synthetic-caregiver-intake-v0.json.
Do not collect or invent real case data; use synthetic-only fixtures.
Do not create treatment, trial, prognosis, monitoring, urgency, or expanded-access advice.
Update metadata, catalog, README, roadmap, and case-to-cure links for any new artifact.
success_criteria: A concrete public-safe next artifact exists and validates.
The artifact is linked from the caregiver intake spec or case-to-cure blueprint.
The run reports the next remaining blocker or next phase without marking the whole initiative complete prematurely.
plans: Prefer the smallest high-value artifact: no-PHI/no-advice intake checklist or private-to-public projection contract.

items.label: Case-to-cure loop governor
items.goal: Make the loop itself enforce next-step synthesis after each completed public-safe step.
requirements: Read the adaptive master plan and update Clawdad/ORP instructions so closed phases flow into the next safest step.
No real case data, PHI, patient-specific advice, treatment ranking, trial advice, or cure claim.
success_criteria: Delegation packet and ORP state point to the adaptive loop, not a closed finite phase.
plans: Start here, then hand off to private intake schema contract or the next revealed public-safe dependency.

items.label: Private intake schema contract
items.goal: Define the private-only intake record shape and public-safe projection boundary for future consented multiple myeloma case work.
requirements: Document field groups, unknown states, provenance, private-only identifiers, and public projection rules without collecting real data.
success_criteria: A public-safe contract or blocker note exists and links to the caregiver intake spec and case-to-cure blueprint.
plans: Prefer a markdown contract first; executable schema can follow only after the public boundary is clear.

items.label: Static synthetic caregiver prototype
items.goal: Design the first synthetic-only public prototype plan for a caregiver-centered intake flow that never accepts real case details.
requirements: Use synthetic fixtures only and keep emergency, clinician-review, consent, privacy, and no-advice copy visible.
success_criteria: Prototype plan or fixture extension exists with public navigation and validation coverage.
plans: Keep it static and synthetic before any backend or data capture.

items.label: Public projection validator
items.goal: Create a fail-closed public projection validator or validator spec for no-PHI, no-advice, synthetic-only public exports.
requirements: Validator must reject identifiers, real case free text, advice language, rankings, urgency guidance, and publication without gate approval.
success_criteria: A validator, validator map, or precise blocker note exists and is linked from case-intake and case-to-cure artifacts.
plans: Start as protocol-level checks if executable validation needs a later schema.

items.label: Consent privacy security retention gate
items.goal: Specify the human and technical gates before any real case intake, storage, retention, emergency handling, or clinician review.
requirements: Public artifact must define gates and blockers only; it must not authorize real intake or collect data.
success_criteria: Gate spec exists or blocker note records the exact private, legal, clinical, and security reviews needed.
plans: Tie to private-to-public workflow, publication gate, and caregiver intake boundary.

items.label: Case feature normalization contract
items.goal: Define the public-safe shape for normalized disease-state and context features used by the private case-to-cure pipeline.
requirements: Include unknown, not-tested, source-missing, provenance, timepoint, and limitation fields without producing prognosis or option ranking.
success_criteria: A normalization contract, schema plan, or blocker note exists and links to the case-feature public summary.
plans: Keep this as shape and boundary first; executable schema follows after field semantics are stable.

items.label: Measurement normalization contract
items.goal: Define public-safe MRD, response, relapse, lab, imaging, method, threshold, sample, and timepoint normalization for private case work.
requirements: Preserve method, threshold, sample, timepoint, durability, endpoint role, source context, and uncertainty; block cure claims.
success_criteria: Measurement contract or blocker note links to MRD glossary and endpoint guardrails.
plans: Use current MRD guardrails as the boundary before creating any fixture or schema update.

items.label: Therapy exposure timeline contract
items.goal: Define the private timeline shape for prior therapies, lines, exposures, responses, toxicities, constraints, and refractory context.
requirements: Public artifact may define fields and safety gates only; it must not infer recommendations, eligibility, sequencing, or ranking.
success_criteria: Therapy exposure contract or blocker note links to treatment taxonomy and immune therapy boundary.
plans: Keep dates and identifiers private; public examples must be synthetic and non-identifying.

items.label: Molecular immune context contract
items.goal: Define the private/public boundary for cytogenetics, genomics, target assays, pathology, flow, immune context, and source validity fields.
requirements: Do not interpret features as patient options; preserve source, method, date/timepoint, uncertainty, and not-tested states.
success_criteria: Context contract or blocker note links to mechanism maps, context modifier map, and source registry.
plans: Capture shape, provenance, and uncertainty before any hypothesis review uses the fields.

items.label: Evidence retrieval packet
items.goal: Define a reproducible public-source evidence packet skeleton for future private case questions without patient matching.
requirements: Include source IDs, query records, freshness checks, limitations, uncertainty, and no-completeness warnings.
success_criteria: Evidence packet skeleton or blocker note links to source registry, ClinicalTrials.gov query protocol, and open research maps.
plans: Do not match, rank, or advise; make the retrieval method auditable.

items.label: Trial therapy landscape non-advice gate
items.goal: Define how trial, therapy, product, target, and access context can be extracted as landscape information without advice.
requirements: Preserve trial, therapy, product, target, jurisdiction, source freshness, access-date, status, limitation, uncertainty, and review fields as landscape context only.

Block availability claims, eligibility claims, patient matching, trial matching, sequencing advice, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, urgency guidance, ranking, and patient-specific interpretation.
success_criteria: Non-advice gate links to the evidence retrieval packet, trial-landscape schema, therapy-record schema, ClinicalTrials.gov query protocol, source registry, therapy exposure contract, and immune therapy boundary.
Landscape fields preserve source freshness, access-date state, jurisdiction, status, uncertainty, limitations, and review status without patient matching or advice.
plans: Completed as Trial Therapy Landscape Non-Advice Gate v0; next safest public step is candidate hypothesis review question framing.

items.label: Candidate hypothesis review question set
items.goal: Represent candidate hypotheses as review questions for qualified humans, not recommendations or rankings.
requirements: Preserve evidence packet IDs, landscape gate status, source context, uncertainty, review lens, blocked uses, and explicit no patient-action output.
Use the evidence retrieval packet, trial therapy landscape non-advice gate, candidate-option scoring rubric, review packet templates, source registry, and publication gate as fail-closed boundaries.

Do not infer recommendations, rankings, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, urgency guidance, availability, eligibility, or patient-specific interpretation.
success_criteria: Question-set artifact or blocker note links to the evidence retrieval packet, trial therapy landscape non-advice gate, candidate-option scoring rubric, and review packet templates.
Candidate hypotheses are framed only as source-scoped questions for qualified human review, never patient options or ranked choices.
The ORP additional queue records what the question set revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Completed as Candidate Hypothesis Review Question Set v0; next safest public step is a multidisciplinary review packet builder skeleton that can copy, reference, omit, or refuse question fields without generating claims or patient-specific outputs.

items.label: Multidisciplinary review packet builder
items.goal: Define the public-safe packet builder skeleton and route rules for multidisciplinary review under private and clinical gates.
requirements: Preserve question IDs, artifact IDs, source IDs, review-lens fields, missing-input blockers, safety boundaries, and no-generated-claims behavior.

Use the candidate hypothesis review question set, review-packet template, review-packet builder manifest spec, route-table schema/tool, evidence retrieval packet, trial/therapy landscape non-advice gate, publication gate, source registry, public projection validator, private intake schema contract, and public safety workflow as boundaries.

Do not assemble real packets, process real case facts, generate biomedical prose, publish patient-specific outputs, recommend, rank, match trials, infer eligibility, infer availability, provide expanded-access or monitoring guidance, authorize publication, or make clinical decisions.
success_criteria: Packet builder skeleton, route rules, or blocker note links to the candidate hypothesis review question set, manifest spec, review-packet template, route-table schema/tool, evidence packet, landscape gate, source registry, and publication gate.
Route behavior is limited to copy, reference, omit, refuse, or mark missing fields; it does not create generated claims, recommendations, rankings, or patient-specific outputs.
The ORP additional queue records what the builder revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Completed as Multidisciplinary Review Packet Builder v0; next safest public step is an expert validation loop that treats issues, outreach roles, and response-ledger states as public-safe dispositions only.

items.label: Expert validation loop
items.goal: Make expert validation issue updates and response dispositions part of the recurring public loop.
requirements: Tie public issue statuses, outreach-map roles, response-ledger dispositions, source correction tasks, and review-lens gaps into the recurring adaptive loop.
Represent any expert response content only as safe dispositions unless public permission and public source context exist.
Preserve issue IDs, artifact IDs, source IDs, review-lens fields, allowed dispositions, next public actions, uncertainty, and blocked-use boundaries.

Do not publish private correspondence, unpublished expert comments, reviewer identity disclosures beyond already-public outreach maps, recommendations, rankings, patient-specific outputs, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, publication authorization, or clinical decisions.
success_criteria: Expert validation loop artifact or blocker note links to issue index, outreach map, response ledger, candidate question set, multidisciplinary builder, source registry, publication gate, and public safety workflow.
All expert validation updates are represented as public-safe statuses, allowed dispositions, source-context-needed notes, or next public tasks without copying private correspondence.
The ORP additional queue records what the expert validation loop revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Completed as Expert Validation Loop v0; next safest public step is the case-to-public learning extraction gate for sanitation, aggregation, source-scope, uncertainty, privacy, and publication boundaries.

items.label: Case-to-public learning extraction gate
items.goal: Define the sanitation, aggregation, source-scope, uncertainty, privacy, review, and publication gate for any future public learning extracted from a private case.
requirements: Only approved, sanitized, non-identifying, non-advisory, source-scoped learning may become public.

Preserve artifact IDs, source IDs, issue IDs, review-lens fields, disposition states, privacy decision state, allowed output type, uncertainty, limitations, blocked-use boundaries, and publication-gate state.
Use the expert validation loop, publication gate, private-to-public workflow, public safety rules, source registry, stage validator map, and pipeline blueprint as boundaries.

Block patient identifiers, raw records, dates tied to a person, single-case claims, private correspondence, unpublished expert comments, patient-specific recommendations, rankings, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, publication authorization, and clinical decisions.
success_criteria: Learning extraction gate or blocker note links to the expert validation loop, publication gate, private-to-public workflow, public safety rules, source registry, stage validator map, and pipeline blueprint.
Gate fields preserve allowed output type, privacy decision state, public source context, uncertainty, limitations, review status, blocked uses, and publication-gate state.
The ORP additional queue records what the extraction gate revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Completed as Case-To-Public Learning Extraction Gate v0; next safest public step is an end-to-end synthetic case dry run that exercises success, omit, refusal, and blocker paths without real case material.

items.label: End-to-end synthetic case dry run
items.goal: Dry-run the complete case-to-cure path with synthetic-only artifacts from caregiver intake through publication gate.
requirements: Use visibly artificial data only and exercise success, omit, refusal, and blocker paths across intake, projection, normalization, evidence retrieval, landscape gating, candidate question routing, review-packet route rules, expert validation disposition plumbing, case-to-public learning extraction, and publication-gate refusal.

Link the synthetic caregiver intake fixture, synthetic case-to-cure pipeline, public projection validator, normalization contracts, evidence retrieval packet, trial/therapy landscape non-advice gate, candidate hypothesis review question set, multidisciplinary review packet builder, expert validation loop, case-to-public learning extraction gate, publication gate, stage validator map, pipeline blueprint, source registry, private-to-public workflow, and public safety rules.

Do not use real case data, identifiers, raw records, uploads, person-linked dates, free-text case details, private correspondence, patient-specific outputs, recommendations, rankings, matching, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, publication authorization, or clinical decisions.
success_criteria: Synthetic dry-run artifact or validation report exists and links all owner artifacts, gates, and blockers.
The dry run demonstrates success, omit, refusal, and blocker paths without processing or resembling a real person.
The ORP additional queue records what the dry run revealed and activates or blocks the master completion audit.
plans: Completed as End-To-End Synthetic Case Dry Run v0; next safest public step is the case-to-cure master completion audit.

items.label: Case-to-cure master completion audit
items.goal: Audit whether the adaptive master plan is complete, safely blocked, or ready for a new named phase.
requirements: Inspect the adaptive master backlog, public artifacts, catalogs, navigation, ORP state, validators, blockers, and remaining human gates.

Do not call the initiative complete unless all remaining work is explicitly expert-review-needed, private-lab-needed, clinical-team-needed, legal-needed, regulatory-needed, publication-gate-needed, human-review-needed, or out-of-scope.
Do not use real case data, clinical judgment, outreach, private lab access, publication authorization, or patient-specific guidance.
success_criteria: Completion audit records files changed, validation state, blockers, and the next named phase or human gate.
The audit states whether the public-safe adaptive scope is complete, safely blocked, or ready for a new named phase.
The ORP additional queue records the audit outcome and next state without requiring real case data or human decisions.
plans: Completed as Case-To-Cure Master Completion Audit v0; it records the public-safe adaptive case-to-cure scope as complete and blocks the next state on named human gates unless a human selects a new named public-safe phase.

items.label: Case-to-cure public-scope human gate blocker
items.goal: Record that no autonomous public-safe case-to-cure backlog item remains after the master completion audit.
requirements: Stop autonomous case-to-cure backlog work unless a human selects a new named public-safe phase or clears a named gate.
Keep remaining work labeled as expert-review-needed, private-lab-needed, clinical-team-needed, legal-needed, regulatory-needed, publication-gate-needed, human-review-needed, or out-of-scope.
Do not create another sibling artifact, tool, schema, or extraction phase by momentum.
Do not use real case data, clinical judgment, outreach, private lab access, publication authorization, credentials, paid services, or patient-specific guidance.
success_criteria: The blocker is explicit in ORP state, additional queue, version stack, inventory, and completion audit.
The next action names human gate classes instead of an autonomous artifact.
Validation passes with the completion audit cataloged.
plans: Blocked until human selection of a new named public-safe phase or clearance of expert-review-needed, private-lab-needed, clinical-team-needed, legal-needed, regulatory-needed, publication-gate-needed, or human-review-needed gates.

items.label: Static synthetic caregiver intake frontend
items.goal: Build the first static, synthetic-only public caregiver intake frontend prototype for multiple myeloma case organization, using existing public artifacts as boundaries and refusing real patient data.
requirements: Do not collect, submit, upload, store, email, or transmit real case data; the prototype must be static and synthetic-only.
Use generic caregiver language only and no private names.
Use the synthetic caregiver fixture and existing intake/product specs as source material.
Keep clinical boundary copy visible: no diagnosis, treatment, trial, prognosis, monitoring, urgency, or expanded-access advice.
Create a usable public first screen rather than a marketing landing page; include frg.earth stewardship.
success_criteria: A static frontend entrypoint exists and works locally without a backend.
The frontend makes it easy to understand the intake sections, unknown/not-sure states, review step, privacy boundary, and private-lab handoff path.
No public artifact contains private names, PHI, real case data, advice outputs, rankings, matching, or cure claims.
Navigation and ORP state are updated and validation passes.
plans: Completed as Static Synthetic Caregiver Intake Frontend v0: a no-build HTML/CSS/JS prototype that uses synthetic fixture content, visible public-safety copy, disabled private handoff controls, and no data submission path.

Next safest public step is a smoke-test report that verifies no-submit, no-storage, no-upload, no-backend, responsive layout, refusal-copy, and no-advice behavior before any future frontend iteration.

items.label: Static synthetic caregiver intake frontend smoke test
items.goal: Document public-safety smoke checks for the static synthetic caregiver intake frontend so the prototype is auditable before any future UI iteration.
requirements: Use the static frontend, metadata, product spec, projection checklist, consent/privacy/security gate, public safety governance, and synthetic fixture only.
Verify that the frontend has no form submission path, no upload control, no backend call, no local storage, no session storage, no cookies, no auth, no email path, and no database behavior.

Check desktop and mobile layouts for readable boundary copy, unknown/not-sure states, final review, emergency boundary, clinician-review boundary, frg.earth stewardship, and disabled private handoff controls.

Do not use real case data, private names, identifiers, records, person-linked dates, free-text case details, patient-specific outputs, recommendations, rankings, matching, treatment guidance, trial guidance, monitoring guidance, expanded-access guidance, publication authorization, outreach, or private lab access.
success_criteria: A public smoke-test report exists and is linked from the frontend metadata and case-intake README.
The report records desktop and mobile checks plus no-submit/no-storage/no-upload/no-backend/no external image dependency behavior using synthetic-only content.
Remaining real intake, private-lab, clinical, legal, publication, outreach, and case-data work remains blocked.
Validation passes after catalog and navigation updates.
plans: Completed as Static Synthetic Caregiver Intake Frontend Smoke Test v0, including static source scans, Playwright desktop/mobile checks, disabled handoff controls, storage/cookie checks, and local image/CSP repair.
It handed off to myeloma-state-object-schema-v0, which is now complete; the active next item is synthetic-myeloma-state-fixture-v0.

items.label: Myeloma state object schema
items.goal: Define the first public, synthetic-only myeloma disease-state object schema derived from the machine representation stack.
requirements: Represent source context, tumor genomic events, transcriptome state, marrow ecosystem state, clinical context, treatment timeline, measurement timeline, missingness, uncertainty, and review status.
Use source-scoped fields and explicit unknown/not-tested/not-collected states; never infer absence from a missing assay or modality.

Keep the artifact public-safe and synthetic-only: no real case data, identifiers, raw records, uploads, exact person-linked dates, report text, free-text case details, model weights, predictions, recommendations, matching, ranking, or clinical decisions.

Link the artifact to the machine representation stack, case-feature normalization contract, measurement normalization contract, therapy exposure timeline contract, molecular immune context contract, evidence retrieval packet, and public safety governance.
success_criteria: A myeloma-state-object-schema-v0 artifact exists with metadata and public navigation links.
The schema separates required source/review metadata from optional modality-specific fields.
The schema includes fail-closed missingness and blocked-use rules.
Validation passes after catalog and ORP updates.
plans: Completed as schemas/myeloma-state-object-schema-v0.md.
Handoff activates synthetic-myeloma-state-fixture-v0 for visibly artificial fixture coverage.

items.label: Synthetic myeloma state fixture
items.goal: Create synthetic-only myeloma state object fixtures that exercise the schema across complete multimodal, missing RNA, missing single-cell, and private-source-blocked scenarios.
requirements: Use the myeloma state object schema, machine representation stack, upstream normalization contracts, evidence retrieval packet, and public safety governance as boundaries.
Every fixture must be visibly artificial and must include source context, missingness, uncertainty, review/gate state, and blocked output manifest fields.

Do not include real case data, identifiers, raw records, exact person-linked dates, report text, uploads, private correspondence, model weights, predictions, recommendations, matching, ranking, or clinical decisions.
success_criteria: A synthetic-myeloma-state-fixture-v0 artifact exists with metadata and public navigation links.
Fixtures exercise complete multimodal, missing RNA, missing single-cell, and private-source-blocked schema paths.
Missingness is explicit and never implies absence, normality, lower risk, safety, treatment fit, trial fit, or no need for review.
Validation passes after catalog and ORP updates.
plans: Completed as examples/synthetic-myeloma-state-fixture-v0.json.
Handoff activates model-output-boundary-wrapper-v0 because every fixture repeats the same blocked-output manifest and prediction-head refusal states.

items.label: Model output boundary wrapper
items.goal: Define reusable public-safe refusal and uncertainty rules for progression, response, MRD, and resistance head placeholders before any model-facing output work.
requirements: Use the myeloma state object schema, synthetic fixture, measurement normalization contract, therapy exposure timeline contract, molecular immune context contract, evidence retrieval packet, and public safety governance as boundaries.
Represent progression, response, MRD, and resistance head placeholders only as not-implemented, calibration-needed, or blocked-patient-specific states.

Do not produce scores, thresholds, probabilities, risk categories, candidate options, matches, rankings, treatment guidance, trial guidance, monitoring guidance, expanded-access guidance, publication authorization, or clinical decisions.
Preserve uncertainty, missingness, review status, gate status, source context, and refusal reason for every placeholder output family.
success_criteria: A model-output-boundary-wrapper-v0 artifact exists with metadata and public navigation links.
The wrapper defines allowed placeholder fields, required refusal states, required uncertainty and review fields, and blocked downstream uses.
The wrapper can be referenced by future validators or fixtures without creating predictions or advice.
Validation passes after catalog and ORP updates.
plans: Completed as disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/model-output-boundary-wrapper-v0.md.
Handoff activates myeloma-state-validator-rule-map-v0 because the schema, fixture, and wrapper now need stable fail-closed rule IDs before any future validator or review packet references them.

items.label: Myeloma state validator rule map
items.goal: Define no-code validator rules for myeloma state objects, synthetic fixtures, and model-output boundary wrapper fields without validating real cases or creating prediction behavior.
requirements: Use the myeloma state object schema, synthetic fixture, model-output boundary wrapper, measurement normalization contract, therapy exposure timeline contract, molecular immune context contract, evidence retrieval packet, and public safety governance as boundaries.
Map rule IDs to owner artifacts, required fields, synthetic coverage, fail-closed action, allowed public successor, review state, and blocked downstream uses.
Do not validate, normalize, infer from, score, predict for, rank, match, publish, or make decisions from real case data.
Do not create treatment guidance, trial guidance, monitoring guidance, expanded-access guidance, publication authorization, model weights, scoring code, prediction APIs, or clinical action language.
success_criteria: A myeloma-state-validator-rule-map-v0 artifact exists with metadata and public navigation links.
The rule map covers schema core fields, missingness states, source context, review gates, synthetic fixture scenarios, and model-output wrapper refusal states.
Every rule has a fail-closed action and blocked-use label before future executable validators or review packets can reference it.
Validation passes after catalog and ORP updates.
plans: Completed as disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/myeloma-state-validator-rule-map-v0.md.

Handoff activates machine-representation-source-extraction-v0 because the rule map identifies architecture claims that need exact public source IDs, source-context gaps, and expert-review status before stronger downstream use.

items.label: Machine representation source extraction
items.goal: Map myeloma machine-representation architecture claims to public source IDs, source-context gaps, extraction status, uncertainty, and expert-review needs without model code, predictions, advice, matching, or ranking.

items.label: Machine representation implementation completion audit
items.goal: Audit whether the human-selected machine-representation implementation phase is complete or whether another public-safe adjacent item remains, without opening issues, sending outreach, requesting private material, validating real cases, producing model outputs, advice, matching, ranking, or publication authorization.
requirements: Use the state object schema, synthetic fixture, model-output boundary wrapper, validator rule map, source extraction table, source-gap task queue, source-gap issue draft packet, adaptive master plan, public inventory, public catalog, and public safety governance as audit inputs.
Decide whether the myeloma-machine-representation-implementation-v0 phase is complete, blocked, or needs another public-safe adjacent item.

Name any remaining work only as public-safe next items or explicit expert-review-needed, private-lab-needed, clinical-team-needed, legal-needed, regulatory-needed, publication-gate-needed, or human-review-needed blockers.
Do not open GitHub issues, send outreach, request private material, validate real cases, rank sources, rank evidence, rank options, create model behavior, or authorize publication.
success_criteria: A machine-representation-implementation-completion-audit-v0 artifact exists with metadata and public navigation links.

Validation passes after catalog and ORP updates.
plans: Completed as disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/machine-representation-implementation-completion-audit-v0.md.

items.label: Machine representation public-scope human gate blocker
items.goal: Record that no autonomous public-safe machine-representation implementation item remains after the completion audit.
requirements: Stop autonomous machine-representation implementation work unless a human selects a new named public-safe phase or clears a named gate.

Keep remaining work labeled as expert-review-needed, private-lab-needed, clinical-team-needed, model-governance-needed, legal-needed, regulatory-needed, publication-gate-needed, human-review-needed, or out-of-scope.
Do not create another sibling schema, fixture, validator, issue draft, model, dashboard, extractor, or tool by momentum.

Do not use real case data, clinical judgment, outreach, private lab access, publication authorization, credentials, paid services, model training, predictions, matching, ranking, or patient-specific guidance.
success_criteria: The blocker is explicit in ORP state, additional queue, version stack, inventory, and completion audit.

Validation passes with the completion audit cataloged.
plans: Blocked until human selection of a new named public-safe phase or clearance of expert-review-needed, private-lab-needed, clinical-team-needed, model-governance-needed, legal-needed, regulatory-needed, publication-gate-needed, or human-review-needed gates.
